visual_attributes.morphology: Anthropomorphic form with the upper body of Vishnu and the lower body of a fish
distinctive_features: Torso of Vishnu with traditional attire
visual_attributes.texture: Scaly on the fish part, smooth on the human part
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Giant fish, large enough to carry a ship
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Upper body of a human connected to the rear half of a fish

domain_specific_visual.VISHNU_ATTRIBUTES: Crown, jewelry, and traditional attire typical of Vishnu

functional_attributes.primary_function: Savior of Manu and humanity from the deluge

domain_specific_functional.SYMBOLISM: Represents the law of dharma and protection of the weak

relationships.description: Matsya is the first avatar of the Hindu god Vishnu.
Matsya saves Manu from the great deluge.
Matsya slays the demon Hayagriva who steals the Vedas.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents divine intervention and protection during times of crisis
